title: Live Virus Dengue - Vero-TMPRSS2 + PGP Inhibitor - Antiviral Screening Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7md89","text":"The following is a protocol for live virus antiviral screening against Dengue in vitro. Cells are prophylactically treated with antiviral candidates that have been serially diluted to investigate activity, via IC50 values generated via analysis of immunofluorescent staining, against Dengue. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lk2r","text":"You may omit PgP inhibtior.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjcm7","text":"Each live virus screening assay is accompanied by a matching cytotoxicity screening assay to investigate drug toxicity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"94lai","text":"Please be sure to wear proper Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) while performing this experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bv6dt","text":"10% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t8b5","text":"2% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11cud","text":"96-well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdbp8","text":"96-well deep well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3eecf","text":"12-channel 200/300ul multichannel pipette","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8itt","text":"200/300ul filtered tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ml95","text":"Compounds of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s3f3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1r3mm","text":"Virus(es) of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crvlv","text":"PPE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dmgh","text":"reservoir","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The serial dilutions will follow different protocols depending on if you will do them by hand or by using a Tecan D300e (Liquid Handler, Tecan, 30100152, https://lifesciences.tecan.com/products/liquid_handling_and_automation/tecan_d300e_digital_dispenser )

Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 according to this diagram: Deep well set up for manual serial dilutions The first row will be the [max] of your compounds. This row will contain 1100ul of 2% media without DMSO . The media for this row does not receive any DMSO since you will add drug dissolved in DMSO directly to it. After filling the first rows, normalize the remainder of the media with DMSO. Fill the remaining wells with 750ul 2% media supplemented with DMSO. These volumes are enough to treat both the antiviral and cytotoxicity plates.

Dilute Dengue stock in 2% media with 2uM elacridar for infection to an MOI of approximately 0.15 . MOI is chosen to facilitate about 30 percent infection in DMSO wells. Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern
Incubate the cells at 37 °C 5% CO2 for 02:00:00 .
Incubate at 37 °C for 6 days
After 6 days, add 100ul 10 % (v/v) Formaldehyde Contributed by users to all wells on the plate.
Incubate for 24:00:00 at room temperature

Image the infected plates on a plate reader. We use a Cytation1 Cytation1 (Imager, Biotek, n/a, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/cell-analysis/cell-imaging-microscopy/cell-imaging-multimode-readers/biotek-cytation-1-cell-imaging-multimode-reader-1623200 )

Dilute Dengue stock in 2% media with 2uM elacridar for infection to an MOI of approximately 0.05 . MOI is chosen to facilitate about 30 percent infection in DMSO wells. Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern
Add 50uL of diluted virus to infected wells of antiviral plate
Incubate at 37 °C for 6 days
Add 100ul 10% Formaldehyde to all wells on the plate. Spray each lid gently with ethanol and replace. Double bag the plates in red biohazard bags to bring them out of the facility.
Incubate for 24:00:00 at room temperature inside the bags.

Create a screening priority list ahead of time. This list will have the compounds of interest, what maximum concentration you'd like to screen at, and where the compounds are located. We recommend organizing the compounds the day before.
Perform the serial dilutions: Add the compounds of interest to the first row of the deep well (containing 1100ul media).
mix well and move 375ul from row 1 to the row 2. This is your first 1:3 dilution. Schematic of the manual serial dilutions
Repeat until the entire deep well has been done. You now have 8 concentrations of your compounds of interest.

Remove the growth media from the plates. Work one pair of plates at a time to prevent drying of the cells. Add 100ul of the serial dilutions to the wells in the following set up: You will perform this for both the infection plate and the matching cytotoxicity plate Treatment layout of the HeLa-ACE2 plates. You should always have the plates in the correct orientation (top left corner should be A1) so that you are sure where the highest and lowest [compound] are.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 : Add 800ul 2% Media to the first row (A) Add 800ul 2% Media supplemented with DMSO to the rest of the rows
You will not be performing any serial dilutions, the tecan will dispense the appropriate amount of drug into each well for you once you set it up. Using the Tecan D300e by Briana L McGovern

Image the infected plates on the Cytation1 Cytation1 (Imager, Biotek, n/a, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/cell-analysis/cell-imaging-microscopy/cell-imaging-multimode-readers/biotek-cytation-1-cell-imaging-multimode-reader-1623200 )
Perform the mock infection on the cytotoxicity plates. Add 50ul 2% media with elacridar to the wells. Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
In parallel, process the cytotoxicity plates. Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
Follow the cyotoxicity protocol for the uninfected plates Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern

The day before your assay, seed 96-well plates with Vero-TMPRSS2 cells at 4,000 cells/well using standard 10% Growth Media . You will need to make one pair of plates for every 3 compounds. One plate is the antiviral plate that will be infected, and the other is the cytoxicity plate that will measure the toxicity of the antiviral candidates. For each pair you can test 3 compounds (in triplicate, 8 dilutions per compound), 2 DMSO controls, and 1 uninfected control in the following layout: Plate Layout Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
Generating Antiviral & Cytotoxicity Curves by Briana L McGovern
Follow the Dengue Antiviral Staining Protocol DENGUE Antiviral Immunofluorescence staining Protocol by Briana L McGovern
Treat the plates with 100ul compound using the deep wells.
Incubate for 2 hours at 37C 5%CO2
use Generating Antiviral & Cytotoxicity Curves by Briana L McGovern
use DENGUE Antiviral Immunofluorescence staining Protocol by Briana L McGovern

Calculate how much media you will need for the entire experiment using the following values in step 5.1 For this assay, the total volume of media that you make will need to be supplemented with 2uM PGP Inhibitor Elacridar. Elacridar should also be present in the media used for the actual infection so take that volume into account as well. You may omit PgP inhibitor if desired.

Calculate how much media you will need for the entire experiment using the following values in step 5.1 For this assay, the total volume of media that you make will need to be supplemented with 2uM PGP Inhibitor Elacridar. Elacridar should also be present in the media used for the actual infection so take that volume into account as well.
Add 50uL of diluted virus to infected wells of antiviral plate
Perform the mock infection on the cytotoxicity plates. Add 50ul 2% media with elacridar to the wells. Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
creator: Briana L McGovern
